title: PDU Outlet-Level Power Draw
spl:
index=power sourcetype="snmp:pdu:outlet"
| eval pct_outlet=if(rated_amps_outlet>0, round(current_amps/rated_amps_outlet*100,1), null())
| where pct_outlet > 85 OR current_amps > 12
| table pdu_id, outlet_id, current_amps, rated_amps_outlet, pct_outlet
| sort -current_amps
description: Per-outlet amps identify rogue devices and balance load across strips before branch trips.
value: Per-outlet amps identify rogue devices and balance load across strips before branch trips.
grandmaExplanation: -outlet amps identify rogue devices and balance load across strips before branch trips ‚Äî so power, cooling, and physical access problems are found before they hurt uptime.
implementation: Poll outlet bank tables on smart PDUs. Map outlet labels from DCIM. Alert on high draw or imbalance vs peer outlets on same strip.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP TA (PDU-MIB, ENCLOSURE-MIB).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="snmp:pdu:outlet"` (outlet_index, current_amps).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Poll outlet bank tables on smart PDUs. Map outlet labels from DCIM. Alert on high draw or imbalance vs peer outlets on same strip.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:pdu:outlet"
| eval pct_outlet=if(rated_amps_outlet>0, round(current_amps/rated_amps_outlet*100,1), null())
| where pct_outlet > 85 OR current_amps > 12
| table pdu_id, outlet_id, current_amps, rated_amps_outlet, pct_outlet
| sort -current_amps
```

Understanding this SPL

**PDU Outlet-Level Power Draw** ‚Äî Per-outlet amps identify rogue devices and balance load across strips before branch trips.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="snmp:pdu:outlet"` (outlet_index, current_amps). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP TA (PDU-MIB, ENCLOSURE-MIB). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: snmp:pdu:outlet. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="snmp:pdu:outlet". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **pct_outlet** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where pct_outlet > 85 OR current_amps > 12` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **PDU Outlet-Level Power Draw**): table pdu_id, outlet_id, current_amps, rated_amps_outlet, pct_outlet
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (outlet draw), Heatmap (PDU √ó outlet), Table (top consumers).
